papers.title: Supercurrent-controlled spontaneous ferromagnetism of magnetic impurities in a spin-orbit-coupled superconductor
papers.authors: Bingke Xiang, Qiushi He, Yishi Lin, Keyi Liang, Zhijie Li, Jinjiang Zhu, Boren Chen, Youfang Wang, Puhua Zhang, Hongxu Yao, Chunxiang Wu, Tingyu Zhou, Minghu Fang, Yao Lu, Ilya Tokatly, Sebastian Bergeret, Yihua Wang
papers.abstract: Nature Communications, Published online: 21 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70968-1In conventional s-wave superconductors, ferromagnetism is strongly suppressed, due to the singlet pairing of the electrons. Here, Xiang, He, Lin, and coauthors find a long-range ferromagnetic order among impurity sites in the spin-orbit coupled Fe(Se,Te) using scanning superconducting quantum interference device microscopy.
papers.reason: Superconductor physics with magnetic impurities and spin-orbit coupling—directly relevant to electron correlations and exotic phases in condensed matter.

papers.title: Proton transfer regulated photocured robust room-temperature phosphorescence from naphthalimide
papers.authors: Aicheng Wang, Haoxuan Wei, Kunquan Lin, Xing Huang, Mingxing Chen, Wentao Bian, Junxiao Wang, Yuzhou Qiao, Bing Fang, Yuxia Zhao, Jianxiang Yu, Meizhen Yin, Yuhua Dai
papers.abstract: Nature Communications, Published online: 21 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70999-8Photocured materials with room temperature phosphorescence are potentially useful but can be challenging to prepare. Here, the authors report the development of such materials by use of naphthalimide as both a chromophore and photoinitiator.
papers.reason: Organic photoluminescence chemistry; tangentially related to photophysics but not core condensed matter research.

papers.title: X-ray preactivated reversible persistent luminescence enables photodynamic immunotherapy of deep tumors
papers.authors: Win Topatana, Yuchao Sun, Tianao Xie, Yiyuan Zhu, Taorui Yang, Ruijing Shen, Peng Ran, Chengao Li, Jiadong Chen, Xuqiu Shen, Ziyi Lu, Yina Han, Yukai Shan, Shijie Li, Tianen Chen, Xiujun Cai, Renren Deng, Sarun Juengpanich, Mingyu Chen
papers.abstract: Nature Communications, Published online: 21 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71028-4Persistent luminescence is promising for photodynamic therapy in deep-seated tumors, but the uncontrollability of light within the body complicates precise spatiotemporal regulation. Here, the authors report X-ray preactivated elimusertib-loaded tumor-targeted photodynamic nanoparticles with controllable “on-off-on” afterglow properties for the treatment of deep-seated tumors.
papers.reason: Luminescent materials for medical applications; peripheral interest in materials properties but not core condensed matter physics focus.

papers.title: Activation of l-histidine biosynthesis as a new antibiotic strategy against Mycobacterium tuberculosis
papers.authors: Debbie M. Hunt, João Pedro Pisco, Angela Rodgers, Cesira de Chiara, Anisha Zaveri, Kamila L. Pacholarz, Dimitrios Evangelopoulos, Acely Garza-Garcia, Sabine Ehrt, Dirk Schnappinger, Perdita E. Barran, Maximiliano G. Gutierrez, Luiz Pedro S. de Carvalho
papers.abstract: Nature Communications, Published online: 21 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70510-3Here, the authors report metabolic activation as a potential antimicrobial strategy against Mycobacterium tuberculosis. They show that supra-physiological activation of L-histidine biosynthesis reduces bacterial growth and causes loss of fitness due to nutrient and energy depletion.
papers.reason: Biochemistry and antibiotic development; outside condensed matter domain.

papers.title: An ultrafast plenoptic-camera system for high-resolution 3D particle tracking in unsegmented scintillators
papers.authors: Till Dieminger, Saúl Alonso-Monsalve, Christoph Alt, Claudio Bruschini, Noemi Bührer, Edoardo Charbon, Kodai Kaneyasu, Tim Weber, Matthew Franks, Davide Sgalaberna
papers.abstract: Nature Communications, Published online: 21 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70918-xWe propose an alternative approach to elementary particle detection: a plenoptic system combined with time-resolving single-photon avalanche diode array sensors leads to ultrafast three-dimensional high-resolution imaging in unsegmented scintillator.
papers.reason: Instrumentation for particle tracking in scintillators; no condensed matter physics content.

papers.title: Cell-type-specific immune programs orchestrate spatial defense in the Arabidopsis leaf epidermis
papers.authors: Jingpu Song, Mahsa Modareszadeh, Dinithi Kumarapeli, Wilson Andres Acosta, Yuhai Cui, Yangdou Wei
papers.abstract: Nature Communications, Published online: 21 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70843-zHere, salicylic acid defenses are found to propagate from infected Arabidopsis cells via calcium-dependent signaling. Guard cells deploy a distinct, salicylic acid-independent immune strategy driven by calcium and reactive oxygen species signaling.
papers.reason: Plant biology and immunity; no relevance to condensed matter or materials physics.

papers.title: In vivo metabolic tagging and targeting of circulating red blood cells
papers.authors: Yusheng Liu, Yizun Wang, Kyungwon Ko, Yuan Liu, Haiyi Huang, Yueji Wang, Jiadiao Zhou, Dhyanesh Baskaran, Joonsu Han, Rimsha Bhatta, Daniel Nguyen, Cecilia Leal, Matthew R. Berry, Fan Lam, Hua Wang
papers.abstract: Nature Communications, Published online: 21 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71013-xStrategies to directly engineer red blood cells (RBCs) in vivo for drug delivery, imaging, and other applications are lacking. Here, the authors develop a strategy for in vivo metabolic tagging of RBCs and show applications in imaging and prolonged blood circulation of therapeutic agents.
papers.reason: Biomedical application of cell tagging; not condensed matter physics.

papers.title: In-utero exposure to chikungunya and child morbimortality: a population-based study using linked routine data
papers.authors: Mio Kushibuchi, Orlagh Carroll, Thiago Cerqueira-Silva, Viviane S. Boaventura, Maria Glória Teixeira, Mauricio L. Barreto, Enny S. Paixão
papers.abstract: Nature Communications, Published online: 21 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70786-5Long-term effects of in utero Chikungunya exposure are not well characterized, although intrapartum exposure is known to be associated with neonatal mortality. Here, the authors assess risk of all-cause hospitalization and mortality up to three years following in-utero exposure in a cohort from Brazil.
papers.reason: Epidemiological study of viral exposure; not relevant to condensed matter or materials physics.

papers.title: Show GN: alogin – TUI, 멀티홉 게이트웨이, 암호화 저장소를 갖춘 SSH 접속 관리 도구 (Go)

papers.title: Azure Entra ID 로그인 로그 우회 취약점 세 번째와 네 번째 사례 공개
papers.abstract: 2023년 이후 Azure Entra ID 로그인 로그 우회 취약점이 총 4건 발견되었으며, 최근 두 건은 정상 토큰 발급이 가능한 심각한 문제로 확인됨 GraphGoblin은 OAuth2 ROPC 흐름에서 scope 파라미터를 반복 입력해 로그 생성을...

papers.title: Wayland가 리눅스 데스크탑을 10년 뒤로 되돌렸는가?
papers.abstract: 1980년대 개발된 X11의 복잡성을 대체하기 위해 등장한 Wayland는 단순한 디스플레이 프로토콜을 목표로 했으나, 17년이 지난 지금도 완성도 논란이 지속됨 전체 시장의 40~60% 수준 채택에 그치며, PipeWire 등 다른 시스템 구...

papers.title: 서버용 웹 기반 그래픽 인터페이스 Cockpit
papers.abstract: 웹 브라우저에서 실행되는 Linux 서버 관리용 그래픽 인터페이스로, 실제 시스템 세션과 직접 상호작용하는 경량형 관리자 도구 시스템 자원, 네트워크, 스토리지, 로그 등을 시각적으로 제어할 수 있는 대화형 관리 환경 제공

papers.title: 일본의 젓가락 사용 금기 용어집
papers.abstract: 일본 식사 예절에서 젓가락 사용 시 피해야 할 행동을 정리한 용어집으로, 이러한 행위는 ‘키라이바시(kiraibashi)’라 불림 젓가락을 입보다 높이 들거나, 국물에 씻거나, 음식을 뒤적이는 등 무례하거나 금기시되는 행동이 다수 포함됨 ...

papers.title: Super Micro 주가, 공동창업자의 25억 달러 규모 AI 칩 밀수 혐의 기소 후 25% 급락

papers.title: 실행 중인 세션에 채널을 통해 이벤트 푸시하기

papers.title: Andrej Karpathy가 말하는 코드 에이전트, AutoResearch, 그리고 AI의 루피(Loopy) 시대 [유튜브]

papers.title: MacBook M5 Pro와 Qwen3.5 기반 로컬 AI 보안 시스템

papers.title: Apple, 인기 '바이브 코딩' 앱 업데이트를 조용히 차단
